qa_pairs.question: What modules are required to be loaded to run a CPMD job using the provided example Slurm scripts?
qa_pairs.answer: To run a CPMD job using the example Slurm scripts, you must load the following modules: `StdEnv/2020`, `intel/2020.1.217`, `openmpi/4.0.3`, and `cpmd/4.3`.

qa_pairs.question: How do you specify the location of pseudo-potentials when running the CPMD program in parallel via an `srun` command in a job script?
qa_pairs.answer: You can specify the location of pseudo-potentials in two ways when running CPMD in parallel: if the input file and pseudo-potentials are in the same directory, use `srun cpmd.x <input files> > <output file>`. If the pseudo-potentials are located in a different directory, use `srun cpmd.x <input files> <path to pseudo potentials location> > <output file>`.
